info.title: Maestro API
info.description: API for triggering and managing automated workflows

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the account.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
Filter workflows by their status. If provided, only workflows with the specified status will be returned.
- `active`: Returns only active workflows.
- `inactive`: Returns only inactive workflows.
- `publishing`: Returns workflows currently being published.
- `unpublishing`: Returns workflows currently being unpublished.
- `archived`: Returns workflows that have been archived.
- `archiving`: Returns workflows currently being archived.

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the account.

get.summary: Gets the details for a specific instance for a deployed workflow definition.
get.description: Gets the details for a specific instance for a deployed workflow definition. The Maestro API is currently only available as part of a beta program. See Get access to Maestro API for details. ### Related topics - [How to get the status of a Maestro workflow instance][howto-status] [howto-status]: /docs/maestro-api/how-to/get-workflow-status/

200.description: Successful response containing the workflow instance details

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the account.

post.description: Cancels a running [workflow][workflows-concept] instance. The Maestro API is currently only available as part of a beta program. See Get access to Maestro API for details. ### Related topics - [How to cancel a Maestro workflow instance][howto-cancel] [howto-cancel]: /docs/maestro-api/how-to/cancel-workflow/ [workflows-concept]: /docs/maestro-api/maestro101/workflows/

AccountId.name: accountId
AccountId.description: The unique identifier of the account.

InstanceId.name: instanceId
InstanceId.description: Unique identifier for the workflow instance

examples: An unexpected error occurred on the server.

code.description: HTTP status code for the error.

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the agreement document was created.

created_by.description: User ID of the person who created the agreement document.

modified_at.description: Timestamp when the agreement document was last modified.

modified_by.description: User ID of the person who last modified the agreement document.

RequestId.description: Unique identifier for the request, useful for tracking and debugging.

ResponseMetadata.description: Additional server-side metadata for a given response

ResponseTimestamp.description: The timestamp indicating when the response was generated.

ResponseDurationMs.description:
The duration of time, in milliseconds, that the server took to process and respond
to the request. This is measured from the time the server received the request
until the time the response was sent.

UUID.description: A universally unique identifier (UUID) in standard 8-4-4-4-12 format.

TriggerInputs.description:
Key-value pairs representing the input data required to trigger the workflow.
The keys correspond to the `field_name` values defined in the `trigger_input_schema` of the workflow definition.
The values should match the specified `field_data_type` (e.g., string, number, boolean).
Example: {"name": "John Doe", "email": "johndoe@example.com"}

additionalProperties.description:
A key-value pair where the key is the `field_name` defined in the `trigger_input_schema` of the workflow definition,
and the value is the actual input data. Supported types include string, number, boolean, object, or array.

oneOf.description: String input, e.g., names, emails, etc.
Numeric input, e.g., ages, IDs, etc.
Nested object input, if a complex structure is required.
An array of values if the input expects multiple items.

TriggerWorkflow.description:
The input information needed to trigger a new instance of a Maestro workflow.
This request body contains the metadata to describe the instance being created,
along with the input data required to trigger the workflow.

- `instance_name` provides a user-defined name for the workflow instance.
- `trigger_inputs` contains the key-value pairs corresponding to the inputs required by the workflow, as described in the `trigger_input_schema` from the workflow definition.

instance_name.description:
A descriptive name for the specific instance of the workflow being triggered.
This is typically used for identification and tracking purposes.
Example: "User Registration Workflow Instance"

WorkflowTriggerRequirements.description: Information about the input requirements and configuration necessary to trigger a specific workflow.

trigger_event_type.description:
The type of event that triggers the workflow. In this case, the workflow is initiated
by an HTTP request. Future iterations may support additional event types beyond HTTP.

trigger_http_config.description:
Configuration details specific to HTTP-triggered workflows. This object describes the
HTTP method and URL that will trigger the workflow, providing the endpoint and method
that should be used to initiate the workflow.

method.description:
The HTTP method used to trigger the workflow. This defines the type of request
that will initiate the workflow (e.g., GET, POST).

url.description:
The URL that is associated with the trigger event. This is the endpoint that must be
called using the specified HTTP method to start the workflow.

trigger_input_schema.description:
A list of input fields that define the structure of the data required to trigger the workflow.
Each item describes a field that must be included in the request when the workflow is triggered.
The schema includes the field name, expected data type, and any default values for the input.

items.description: Array of fields required as inputs to trigger the workflow, including field names, data types, and default values.

field_name.description:
The name of the input field expected by the workflow. This key must match the name
provided in the `trigger_inputs` when triggering the workflow.

field_data_type.description:
The data type expected for the input field. This indicates whether the input should
be a string, number, boolean, object, or array, ensuring the data is passed in the
correct format.

default_value.description:
The default value for the input field if one is provided. This can be a string, number,
boolean, object, or array. If no value is provided during the trigger, the workflow may
use this default value.

metadata.description:
Metadata related to the workflow definition, including details about when the workflow was
created, who created it, when it was last modified, and by whom. This information is useful
for tracking changes to the workflow over time and identifying the user or system responsible
for creating or modifying the workflow.

id.description: A unique ID for this workflow

name.description: A user-provided name for this workflow

account_id.description: A unique ID for the account associated with the workflow

status.description: Indicates the readiness and deployment status of a workflow

id.description: Unique identifier for the workflow instance

name.description: Human-readable name for the workflow instance

workflow_status.description: Current status of the workflow (e.g. In Progress, Completed, Canceled)

template_id.description: Identifier linking this instance to a workflow template

account_id.description: Account under which this workflow instance was initiated

started_at.description: Date and time when the workflow was started

started_by.description: User or system identifier that started this workflow

started_by_name.description: Display name of the user who started this workflow

started_by_role.description: Role of the user who started this workflow (e.g. Preparer)

ended_at.description: Date and time when the workflow completed

expires_at.description: Date and time after which the workflow expires

last_modified_at.description: Date and time when the workflow instance was last modified

canceled_at.description: Date and time when the workflow was canceled (if applicable)

canceled_by.description: User or system identifier that canceled this workflow (if applicable)

total_steps.description: Total number of steps configured in the workflow

last_completed_step.description: The index of the most recently completed step

last_completed_step_name.description: The name of the most recently completed step

tags.description: Custom tags for organization or filtering

metadata.description: Additional metadata related to this workflow instance

workflow_created_by.description: Identifier of the user who originally created the workflow definition

workflow_version.description: Version string of the deployed workflow

workflow_metadata_id.description: Identifier for workflow definition metadata in the system

WorkflowInstanceInfo.description:
Represents an instance of a Maestro workflow. This object contains details about a
specific workflow instance, including its unique identifier and a URL that allows
users to access or interact with the running instance.

instance_id.description:
A unique identifier for this specific instance of the workflow. This UUID
is used to reference the running instance in other API calls (e.g., to
retrieve its status or terminate the instance).
Example: '1a46ccde-9db4-42d6-94fa-0ddd07a3d2ff'

instance_url.description:
A fully-qualified URL that can be used to access or interact with this
workflow instance while it is running. This URL may provide access to
additional details, actions, or logs related to the workflow execution.

WorkflowInstanceCreationState.description:
Represents the new state of a workflow's mechanism to permit new workflow instances from being created.
Valid values include:
  - active
  - paused

BadRequest.description: Bad Request - The request could not be understood or was missing required parameters.

Forbidden.description: Forbidden - The request was valid, but you do not have permission to perform the action.

NotFound.description: Not Found - The requested resource could not be found.

Unauthorized.description: Unauthorized - Authentication is required and has failed or not been provided.

examples: Unauthorized - Please provide a valid access token.

Conflict.description: Bad Request - The request conflicts with the current state of the resource.

CancelWorkflowInstanceResponse.description: A message confirming the instance was canceled, including the instance and workflow IDs

PauseNewWorkflowInstancesSuccess.description:
Indicates that a workflow has been successfully paused. New instances of this workflow will not be created.
Existing workflow instances will be unaffected.

ResumeNewWorkflowInstancesSuccess.description:
Indicates that the ability to create new workflow instances from this workflow has been resumed.
Existing workflow instances will be unaffected.

WorkflowsListSuccess.description: A list of workflows has been successfully returned.

TriggerWorkflowSuccess.description: Indicates a trigger has successfully launched a workflow instance

WorkflowTriggerRequirementsSuccess.description: Describes the requirements for triggering a workflow

schema.description:
The input information needed to trigger a new instance of a Maestro workflow.
This request body contains the metadata to describe the instance being created,
along with the input data required to trigger the workflow.

- `instance_name` provides a user-defined name for the workflow instance.
- `trigger_inputs` contains the key-value pairs corresponding to the inputs required by the workflow, as described in the `trigger_input_schema` from the workflow definition.

value.message: Successfully cancelled the workflow instance {instanceId} in {workflowId}

PauseNewWorkflowInstancesExample.summary: The workflow state has been successfully updated to resume creation of new instances

ResumeNewWorkflowInstancesExample.summary: The workflow state has been successfully updated to resume creation of new instances

MaestroOAuth2.description:
OAuth2 authentication.
- Demo:
  - authorizationUrl: https://account-d.docusign.com/oauth/auth
  - tokenUrl: https://account-d.docusign.com/oauth/token
- Production:
  - authorizationUrl: https://account.docusign.com/oauth/auth
  - tokenUrl: https://account.docusign.com/oauth/token

scopes.adm_store_unified_repo_read: Allows read access to the unified repository.
scopes.aow_manage: Allows management of advanced workflow operations.
